hallucinations.description: The paper title, abstract, and framing repeatedly refer to a 'DPO-aligned' model and 'DPO-Aligned Code Generation Models', but the actual model compared is CodeLlama-7B-Instruct (instruction-tuned, not trained with DPO or preference optimization). The code and paper itself acknowledge this ('not specifically trained with code-focused DPO'), making the DPO framing a misrepresentation of the methodology.
hallucinations.evidence: Paper title: 'Error Type Distributions Differ Between RL-Aligned and DPO-Aligned Code Generation Models'; Section 3.1: 'CodeLlama-7B-Instruct (instruction-tuned)... It is a general instruction-following model, not specifically trained with code-focused DPO.'; code/config.py and generate.py label it as 'dpo_model_id' but load 'codellama/CodeLlama-7b-Instruct-hf' with no DPO training.

hallucinations.description: Reference to a nonexistent ICSE 2025 paper: 'The ICSE 2025 study by Wang et al. (2025) provides a three-tier classification based on execution semantics.' No such paper exists; the taxonomy is a custom implementation in the code labeled 'ICSE 2025 Error Taxonomy'.
hallucinations.evidence: Paper Section 2.3 and 3.3; code/analyze.py: 'ICSE 2025 Error Taxonomy' with custom SYNTAX_ERRORS/RUNTIME_ERRORS lists.

overall_assessment: Hallucinations found: primarily a misleading methodology framing (DPO vs. instruction tuning) and at least one nonexistent citation. Experimental results and statistics appear genuine and match the provided code execution logs.
